acceptanceCriteria.evidence: App Server protocol test emitted exactly one turn/steer at 100 uncached tokens and one turn/interrupt at 200, returning budget_paused.
acceptanceCriteria.criterion: A synthetic over-budget turn checkpoints and stops within the configured bound

acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Paused implementation bypasses DeliveryCoordinator, and the scheduler reuses the existing retry ordinal so GameApi start_execution resolves the same AgentRun and Attempt.
acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The same AgentRun resumes without duplicate PR, Evidence or Review artifacts

acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Runtime snapshots expose soft_limit and hard_limit from budget events and provider_rate_limit from normalized retry errors.
acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Status distinguishes soft-limit, hard-limit and provider-rate-limit pauses

acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Soft completion returns a pause and hard budget uses official turn/interrupt; no continuation or review turn is launched by the paused worker.
acceptanceCriteria.criterion: A stable implementation does not continue consuming tokens after all required gates complete

acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Local gate: 341 tests, 100 percent coverage, Credo/specs clean, Dialyzer zero. Exact-head GitHub checks make-all and validate-pr-description succeeded.
acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The full Symphony gate passes with 100 percent coverage and zero Dialyzer findings

residualRisk.risk: Intermediate token enforcement depends on usage notifications.
residualRisk.mitigation: The independent wall-clock hard limit remains authoritative when usage telemetry is delayed.

summary: Symphony now checkpoints and bounds Codex turns by role and uncached context while preserving durable run identity and preventing duplicate delivery work.
